spec_match.rationale: The primary flow now builds a real <usermedia> element with a permissionconstraints child, streamready/error listeners, and getUserMedia only as the fallback comparison.

interactive.rationale: Users can switch between <usermedia> and getUserMedia, probe media devices, request a stream, and simulate the mid-session track-ended recovery path.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index card now describes the <usermedia> element, device probe, permission state transitions, and track-ended recovery that the page actually renders.

edge_cases.rationale: The page probes enumerateDevices for the NotFoundError risk before the user clicks and attaches MediaStreamTrack ended listeners for revoked or removed devices.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links the correct ChromeStatus feature ID 4926233538330624 plus the usermedia explainer.

design_system.rationale: The previous unicode arrows were replaced with inline SVG arrows and all local button background rules set explicit text colors.

summary: Resolved: the camera permission flow is now a <usermedia>-specific onboarding demo with the correct ChromeStatus reference, inline SVG arrows, device probing, and track-ended recovery.
